evals.prompt:
Process these Todoist inbox items:

1. "cancel whispr flow"
2. "There's like this force in all of us that says I'm not good enough. It's like a stop gap to keep the world from chaos. But it can be factored out."
3. "Figure out if I can do some kind of QSBS with compose or something"
4. "Follow up with raiya"
5. "look at brex for everything ai i don't want to run out on that as well"
6. "i guess temporal offers billing through aws marketplace"
7. "feedback from anar -- my site for puffin bot made it look simpler than clawdbot"
8. "send anar an intro request for quiet"

Active projects: Everything AI, Demo Day, Manage Finances
evals.expected_output: A triage report classifying all 8 items. Items 1, 4, 5, 8 are clear actions (imperative language with specific targets). Item 2 is a philosophical reflection (Reference). Item 3 has speculative 'if I can' language but contains a specific financial task. Item 6 is an observation about a tool (Reference). Item 7 is feedback to remember (Reference).
expectations: Each of the 8 items is classified into exactly one category (Action, Reference, Project Seed, or Clarify)
'cancel whispr flow' is classified as Action (imperative verb + specific target)
'Follow up with raiya' is classified as Action (imperative + person)
'send anar an intro request for quiet' is classified as Action (imperative + person + specific request)
The philosophical reflection (item 2) is NOT classified as Action — it's Reference or Project Seed
'i guess temporal offers billing through aws marketplace' is NOT classified as Action — it's an observation (Reference)
'feedback from anar' is classified as Reference (information to preserve, not actionable right now)
Original inbox text is preserved verbatim for each item
A triage report is produced with summary stats
Action items have verb-first titles and project assignments

evals.prompt:
Process these Todoist inbox items:

1. "two orchestration frameworks" with description containing links to github.com/glittercowboy/get-shit-done and github.com/othmanadi/planning-with-files
2. "one way to go about it would be to basically research a bunch of these" with description linking to github.com/automazeio/ccpm
3. "there are so many openclaws and shit" with description linking to a tweet about Claude Code tools
4. "security feature from anar" with description about OpenClaw security docs and prompt injection mitigation
5. "Spec for EAI" with description about craft editor + planning loops + cloud onboarding

Active projects: Everything AI, Demo Day, Manage Finances
evals.expected_output: Items 1-3 should cluster as they're all about Claude Code orchestration/project management tools. Item 4 is a detailed feature discussion (Project Seed or Reference with Everything AI relevance). Item 5 is a project seed or action for Everything AI. The cluster should produce one reference note, not three.
expectations: Items 1, 2, and 3 (orchestration tools/Claude Code tooling) are clustered into one reference note rather than filed separately
The clustered note groups them by problem domain (orchestration/project management), not by source
Item 4 (security feature discussion) is identified as relevant to Everything AI
Item 5 (Spec for EAI) is connected to Everything AI project
A MOC is suggested if 3+ items cluster under one topic
GitHub/link descriptions are concise (what it does + activity), not full README summaries

evals.prompt:
Process these Todoist inbox items:

1. "Could make the bot for the event through the text api"
2. "let chen-chen know about the space"
3. "Puffin as a journey and character with waycraft"
4. "linq is a pretty good business as well"
5. "Let me either use the text bot or vibe code a voting tool"
6. "make sure the semantic search or whatever setting is enabled as part of onboarding"

Active projects: Everything AI, Demo Day, Manage Finances
evals.expected_output: Item 1 uses 'Could make' (modal) — brainstorming, NOT action. Item 2 is a clear action (imperative + person). Item 3 is ideation about a product concept. Item 4 is an observation. Item 5 has 'Let me' which is closer to intent but still exploratory. Item 6 is a specific task for EAI onboarding.
expectations: 'Could make the bot for the event through the text api' is NOT classified as Action (modal 'could' = brainstorming)
'let chen-chen know about the space' IS classified as Action (imperative + person)
'Puffin as a journey and character with waycraft' is classified as Reference or Project Seed (ideation, no verb)
'linq is a pretty good business as well' is NOT classified as Action (observation/reference)
'make sure the semantic search or whatever setting is enabled as part of onboarding' is classified as Action (specific task for EAI)
Items correctly distinguish between voice-capture ideation (modal language) and genuine action items (imperative + target)
Project assignments leverage domain context (EAI-related items → Everything AI)
Triage report includes a Clarify section for any items where confidence is below 80%

evals.prompt:
Process these Todoist inbox items:

1. "check this out" with link to https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook
2. "interesting agent framework" with link to https://github.com/microsoft/autogen
3. "tool for running claude in ci" with link to https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action
4. "tell raiya about the autogen thing"
5. "I wonder if we could use something like this for the onboarding flow" (referring to item 3)

Active projects: Everything AI, Demo Day, Manage Finances
evals.expected_output: Items 1-3 are GitHub link captures. Each should get a concise summary (one sentence on what it does, activity signals like stars/last-commit) — NOT a full README summary or multi-paragraph description. Item 4 is an action (imperative + person). Item 5 is voice-capture ideation (modal 'I wonder if we could').
expectations: GitHub repo summaries are concise: each is 1-3 sentences max describing what the tool does and its activity level (stars, maintenance status)
GitHub summaries do NOT include full feature lists, installation instructions, or multi-paragraph README content
Item 1 (anthropic-cookbook) is classified as Reference
Item 2 (autogen) is classified as Reference
Item 3 (claude-code-action) is classified as Reference and noted as relevant to Everything AI
Item 4 ('tell raiya about the autogen thing') is classified as Action with Everything AI project (Raiya is EAI contact)
Item 5 ('I wonder if we could use...') is NOT classified as Action — it's ideation/brainstorming (modal language)
Items 1-3 are considered for clustering since they're all developer tools in the AI/agent ecosystem
